title: The Beginning
subtitle: Where it all started
story:
It all started at Bangalore Airport, where we met for the first time before heading to Vietnam on different flights. We introduced ourselves, knowing we'd all be spending the next few days together. 

The real beginning, though, was in Vietnam.

That morning, we stood with everyone else, deciding what coffee to order. Looking back, it's funny how that was the first of so many coffees we'd end up sharing.

Later that evening, we talked a little more, laughed a little more, and somewhere between those ordinary moments, our story quietly began.

title: Our Conversations
subtitle: Late nights, long replies
story:
Those eight days in Vietnam gave us some of my favorite memories.

There were conversations with everyone around us, laughter shared as a group, and then there were the conversations that slowly became just ours. Somewhere between sightseeing, coffee breaks, and long days, talking to you became the easiest thing in the world.

You took such good care of me throughout the trip in ways both big and small, and I'll always remember that. And then came the last night—the first time we held hands. A simple moment that somehow said more than words ever could.

Back in Bangalore, the conversations never really ended. They turned into movies, dates, long drives, and countless cups of coffee. Before I knew it, I was sharing the parts of my heart I usually keep hidden, and you quietly became the person I could trust without hesitation.

We spoke about life, dreams, and the future. You lectured me quite a lot too (and yes, I deserved at least some of them 😛). Somehow every conversation left me with a different perspective.

And then there were the completely ridiculous ones—the ones where we'd gossip about random people we saw, judge strangers for absolutely no reason, debate cars, laugh over the silliest things, and somehow spend hours talking about absolutely nothing.

Our Sakleshpur and Ooty trips gave us even more of those conversations. We spoke about life, the choices we make, the people we become, and so many things that I'll carry with me for a long time. Those conversations genuinely helped me understand life a little better and, in many ways, helped me live a little better too.

I know I can be stubborn and probably a little annoying sometimes, but I also know that everything you've ever told me came from a place of care. You always wanted the best for me, and I know your words will continue to help me long after the conversation ends.

title: The Little Things
subtitle: The little habits I fell in love with
story:
People often notice the big things about someone. But I think it's the little things that truly make a person unforgettable.

I love the way you always make sure I'm comfortable, whether it's while driving, choosing where we sit, or simply making sure I've eaten. I also love the way you drive... and you do drive me crazy as well. 😉

I love that you always serve me first before serving yourself. That small gesture somehow says so much about you.

I love how you sing along to songs while driving, with your tiny dance moves in between.

I love how observant you are. Even in a room full of people, you somehow always knew where I was or whether I was okay.

I love how you can have the deepest conversations one moment and laugh about something completely ridiculous the next.

These aren't grand gestures or extraordinary moments. They're just little pieces of who you are. And somehow, those little things are exactly what made you so special to me.

title: Favorite Memories
subtitle: Moments I keep close
story:
If someone asked me to pick my favorite memories with you, I don't think I ever could. But these would definitely make the list.

Our Sakleshpur trip and stay, where every drive and every view felt a little more special because we were together.

Our first movie date, where you barely watched the movie... and I only pretended to. 😉

The first time I got drunk, and somehow you made me feel completely safe through it all.

Our dance at Social Koramangala—a moment I'll probably smile about for a very long time.

Our Ladakh trip deserves a chapter of its own. Watching our first snowfall together felt almost magical. The ATV ride was one adventure I'll never forget, and my favorite bike ride ever will always be the one with you.

And of course, us constantly fighting, teasing, and annoying each other over the smallest things. Somehow, even those moments became memories I wouldn't trade for anything.

Then there are all the little adventures, coffees, long drives, random plans, and conversations that never made it into photographs but somehow became some of my favorite memories anyway.

There are so many more that I couldn't possibly fit into one page... and maybe that's the best part of our story.

title: The Person You Are
subtitle: The qualities that make you, you
story:
There are so many things I admire about you, not because you try to be extraordinary, but because this is simply who you are.

The way you always take care of everyone around you without ever expecting anything in return. The love and respect you have for your family, and the way you always put them first. The kind of friend you are—always present, always dependable, and always making people feel like they have someone to count on.

I admire how you appreciate the smallest gestures, how you're kind not just to the people you know but even to complete strangers, and how you choose compassion even when it's easier not to.

I admire your patience, especially with me. Even when you lecture me, I know it always comes from a place of care and because you genuinely want the best for me. Thank you for always making me feel loved, even when you never had to.

I hope you never lose these parts of yourself, because they're what make you, you.

title: Birthday Wishes
subtitle: Everything I wish for you
story:
On your birthday, my biggest wish is that life gives you everything you've worked so hard for.

I hope you get that Google offer you've always dreamed of or start your business and become my richest friend. I hope you buy yourself a new car—I'd secretly love for it to be an Audi. I hope you travel to beautiful places, make unforgettable memories, and truly live life to the fullest.

If getting married is what your heart truly wants, then I hope you find someone who understands you, stands by you, and loves you for the incredible person you are. You deserve nothing less.

No matter where life takes us, I'll always be one of the people quietly cheering for you. Whether I'm a part of your life or supporting you from afar, I'll always hope, pray, and wish for your happiness, your success, and everything beautiful that life has to offer.

Happy Birthday. Thank you for being one of the most wonderful parts of my story. ❤️
